questions.prompt: Which is the most precise definition of an algorithm?
choices: Any program a computer can run
A finite, unambiguous, effective procedure that transforms input into output
A mathematical formula expressed in code
A flowchart describing a process

questions.prompt: The 'state' of a running program is best described as:
choices: Its source code
The values of all its variables plus its current position in the code
The output it has printed so far
The contents of the CPU's instruction set

questions.prompt: Why is the stored-program concept foundational?
choices: Programs live in memory as data, so programs can read, write, and be treated as programs/data interchangeably
It makes programs execute faster than hardwired logic
It guarantees programs cannot be modified at runtime
It reduces the amount of memory computers need

questions.prompt: An algorithm that examines every pair of items in a list of n items performs roughly how many steps?
choices: n
n²
log n
a constant number
The same input always produces the same steps and the same result
It is guaranteed to terminate on every input
It produces output without using randomness anywhere in the universe
It runs in polynomial time
